ARTICLE_TITLE: Son Heung-min Leads Tottenham to Champions League Semi-Final Victory
ARTICLE_BODY: Captain Son Heung-min delivered a masterclass performance as Tottenham Hotspur secured their place in the Champions League final with a thrilling 3-1 victory over Bayern Munich at Wembley Stadium. The South Korean striker scored twice in the second half, including a stunning curler from outside the penalty area that left the German goalkeeper helpless. Son's clinical finishing and leadership on the pitch have been instrumental in Tottenham's remarkable European campaign this season. The 34-year-old forward's goals took his Champions League tally to 12 for the season, making him the tournament's joint top scorer. Korean fans celebrated nationwide as Son became the first Asian player to reach a Champions League final as captain. His emotional post-match interview, where he dedicated the victory to Korean football supporters, went viral across social media platforms. Tottenham will face Manchester City in the final on June 1st at Istanbul's Atatürk Stadium.
